title: Flow_Field_and_Combustion_Characterization_of_Premixed_Gas_Turbine_Flames_by_Pla
text:

Okay, here is the extraction of technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Δp (burner pressure drops): 1% and 3% of absolute pressure upstream of burner

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   Table 1 is referenced in the text, and it contains details of the measuring technique used for the OH* chemiluminescence with the same detection system as used for PLIF. But the table itself is not included in the provided text and images.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1:** Cut-away drawing of the combustor.
    *   Labels: preheated seeding air, fuel, preheated air, radial swirler, exhaust, exhaust gas transition duct, metal side wall, quartz glass window.
*   **Fig. 2:** Experimental setup for the imaging of the OH PLIF and the OH* chemiluminescence.
    *   Labels: concave cylindrical lens, convex lens, aperture, ICCD camera, dye cell, filter, beam splitter, laser system, matrix burner, combustor, video camera, convex lens, photomultiplier, ICCD filter camera, beam splitter.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Thermal power of combustor: 1 MW
*   Air preheating temperature: 400°C
*   Seeding particle diameter: 1 µm
*   Seeded air mass flow rate: up to 4 g/s
*   Pressure: p between 1.5 bars and 6 bars
*   Dye laser wavelength: 283.55 nm (resonant with the Q1(8) line of the A-X (1-0) band of the OH radical)
*   Spectral bandpass filter transparency for wavelengths between 300 nm and 325 nm.
*   Distance between lenses: 64 mm
*   First Lens: f = -50 mm (cylindrical)
*   Second Lens: f = 750 mm (spherical)

title: Flow_Field_and_Combustion_Characterization_of_Premixed_Gas_Turbine_Flames_by_Pla
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   f1 = -12.7 mm, f2 = 200 mm (focal lengths of cylindrical lenses)
*   f = 1000 mm (focal length of spherical lens)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Technical details of the PLIF and Chemiluminescence diagnostics**
    *Column 1: OH* chemiluminescence measurement*
        *   Exposure time: 40 μm
        *   Frame rate: 3 Hz
        *   Effective image size: 512x446 pixels
        *   Bandpass filter: 300-325 nm
    *Column 2: OH PLIF measurement*
        *   Exposure time: 400 ns
        *   Laser pulse duration: 8 ns
        *   Laser pulse energy: 4.6-5.5 mJ
        *   Laser wavelength: 283.55 nm
        *   Effective image size: 359x446 pixels
        *   Bandpass filter: 300-325 nm
        *   Dynamic range: 16 bits
        *   CCD chip size: 512x512 pixels
        *   Objective (flame): UV Halle, 100 mm, f/2
        *   Objective (dye cell): UV Nikkor, 105 mm, f/4.5

*   **Table 2: Technical details of the PIV setup**
    *Column 1: PIV measurement*
        *   Laser pulse duration: 5 ns
        *   Laser pulse energy: 120 mJ
        *   Laser sheet width: 1 mm
        *   Double-pulse delay: 10-15 μs
        *   PIV repetition rate: 5 Hz
        *   Seeding material: TiO2 (1 μm)
        *   PIV camera objective: Nikkor, 50 mm, f/5.6

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 3: Experimental setup for the PIV.**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Beam expansion (cylindrical lenses)
        *   Convex lens
        *   Aperture
        *   ICCD camera
        *   Video camera
        *   Filter (532 nm)
        *   CCD camera for PIV
        *   PIV laser
        *   Filter (300-325 nm)
        *   Combustor

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Wavelength of laser: λ=532 nm
*   Interrogation window size: 12x12 pixels
*   Accuracy of single-shot velocity estimates: ±0.7 m/s
*   Subpixel accuracy of particle displacement: 0.1 pixel
*   Combustion chamber temperature: above 1600°C
*   Interior window temperature: up to 1000°C

title: Flow_Field_and_Combustion_Characterization_of_Premixed_Gas_Turbine_Flames_by_Pla
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   No explicit mathematical equations are present in the text.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4:** Planar flow velocity vs. position
    *   Axes: (Qualitative) position within the imaged region
    *   Units: Flow velocity magnitude is color-coded, from 0 (black), through 50% of max, up to max (white)
    *   Curves: Streamlines showing flow direction
    *   Key Values: Successive instantaneous Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV) measurements at p=3 bars, Δp = 1%, and Φ = 0.59

*   **Figure 5:** OH chemiluminescence intensity vs. position
    *   Axes: Position within the imaged region
    *   Units: Intensity is color-coded, from 0 (black), through 50% of max, up to max (white)
    *   Curves: Spatial distribution of OH* emission
    *   Key Values: Instantaneous distribution of the OH* chemiluminescence (line-of-sight) at p=4 bars

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figures 4 (a), (b), (c):** Planar flow velocity of the combustor visualized through streamlines. Arrows indicate flow direction in each image.

*   **Figure 5:** Instantaneous OH* chemiluminescence. Burner nozzle represented by a white ellipse.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   p = 3 bars (Pressure value for Fig. 4)
*   Δp = 1% (Pressure variation for Fig. 4 and 5)
*   Φ = 0.59 (Equivalence ratio for Fig. 4)
*   p = 4 bars (Pressure value for Fig. 5)
*   Φ =0.55 (Equivalence ratio for Fig. 5)
*   Frame rate: 3-5 Hz
*   PIV repetition rate: 5 Hz
*   Vortex size: 1 cm to 5 cm
*   Heat release location: 5 cm to 8 cm downstream of the burner exit
* Averaged image composed of 200 single shots.

title: Flow_Field_and_Combustion_Characterization_of_Premixed_Gas_Turbine_Flames_by_Pla
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Δp=1.1% at p=1.5 bars
*   Φ=0.53

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 6(a) and 6(b): Single-shot OH PLIF mappings**
    *   **Axes:** No explicit axes labels
    *   **Units:**
        *   OH PLIF intensity: 0 to max, scale indicating 0%, 50%, max.
    *   **Curves:** Represent spatially-resolved OH PLIF intensity.
    *   **Key Values/Parameters:**
        *   (a) p=1.5 bars, Δp=1.1%, Φ=0.53
        *   (b) p=4 bars, Δp=3%, Φ=0.50
*   **Fig. 7: Absolute gradient of OH PLIF intensity.**
    *   **Axes:** No explicit axes labels
    *   **Units:**
        * absolute gradient of OH PLIF intensity: 0 to max, scale indicating 0%, -50%, max.
    *   **Curves:** Represents spatially-resolved gradient of OH PLIF intensity
    *   **Key Values/Parameters:**
        *   p=4 bars (operating point)
*   **Fig. 8: Mean flow field (sum of correlation)**
    *Background: contour plot of the corresponding mean OH density:
        *   Arrows indicate flow direction. Background is contour plot of OH density (qualitative)
    *   **Axes:** Not explicitly defined. Likely spatial axes.
    *   **Units:** Not explicitly defined for flow vectors or contour plot, presumed to be OH density.
    *   **Key Values/Parameters:** p=4 bars, Δp=1%, Φ=0.56

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 8: Mean flow field:** Contains flow vectors.
*   **Fig. 7: Representation of laser sheets:**
    *   Components: "1st sheet position", "2nd sheet position".

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Temperature: 1400 K
*   Time: milliseconds. 0.33 s (interval between images)
*   Pressure: p=1 bar, p=1.5 bars, p=4 bars
*   Pressure drop: Δp=1.1%, Δp=3%, Δp=1%
*   Equivalence Ratio: Φ=0.53, Φ=0.50, Φ=0.56

title: Flow_Field_and_Combustion_Characterization_of_Premixed_Gas_Turbine_Flames_by_Pla
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 10:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Radial position r
        *   y-axis: axial flow speed v_ax [a.u.]
    *   Curves:
        *   Δp = 3%
        *   Δp = 1%
    *   Key values: Values of axial flow speed at different radial positions for Δp = 1% and 3% shown on the graph. The graphs show the distribution of the axial component of the flow velocity averaged over an axial range of 7-16 mm downstream of the burner exit.

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 9:** Diagram indicating planar flow direction with markings: 0°, 90°, -90°, 180°.
*   **Figure 11:**
    *   Diagram shows flow velocity vectors overlaid on a grayscale image representing absolute RMS of the planar flow velocity.
    *   Scale for the grayscale image indicates values from 0% to max, 50% shown.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Axial range: 7-16 mm downstream of the burner.
*   p = 3 bars, Δp = 1%, Φ = 0.59 for flow velocity standard deviation mapping (Figure 11).
*   p = 4 bars, Φ ≈ 0.50 for the chemiluminescence distribution (Figure 12).
* 9 x 9 pixel² smoothing filter.
* Axial range of ±3 mm integration.
* Δρ=2%, Φ=0.53
* 22 mm from the burner, distance to study concentration of the OH
* Pressure below 4 bars
